examples.text: وَمَثَلُ كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ كَشَجَرَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ ٱجْتُثَّتْ مِنْ فَوْقِ الْأَرْضِ مَا لَهَا مِنْ قَرَارٍ
examples.english: And the parable of an evil word is as an evil tree pulled up from the earth's surface; it has no stability.

examples.text:
Ibn Malik
كَلَامُنَا لَفْظٌ مُفِيدٌ كَٱسْتَقِمْ / وَٱسْمٌ وَفِعْلٌ ثُمَّ حَرْفٌ ٱلْكَلِمْ
kalāmunā lafẓun mufīdun kastaqim / wasmun wafiʕlun ṯumma ḥarfun l-kalim
examples.english: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

examples.text:
John 1:1, Van Dyck version
فِي الْبَدْءِ كَانَ الْكَلِمَةُ
fī l-badʔi kāna al-kalimatu
examples.english: In the beginning was the Word

etymology.text: From the root ك ل م (k l m). The Christian meaning is semantic loan from Koine Greek λόγος (lógos).

body.text: An assertion: an expression of opinion.
